Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows markedly elevated creatinine and proteinuria, indicating significant renal impairment, likely chronic kidney disease. Urine findings (cloudy, brown, low specific gravity, proteinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria) further support renal dysfunction. Low HbA1c suggests no chronic hyperglycemia, but glucosuria may indicate tubular dysfunction. Mild metabolic acidosis is present (low CO₂). Recommend urgent nephrology evaluation and further assessment for underlying etiology.
